channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Decline of Classical Greek Studies at Xavier College and Across Victoria
item.description: Victorian students interested in studying classical Greek face dwindling opportunities as Xavier College in Kew ends its 147-year tradition of offering the subject due to persistently low enrolments. ...

item.title: Planned Gas-Powered Data Centers in the U.S. May Significantly Increase Greenhouse Gas Emissions
item.description: A recent report highlights that just 11 planned gas-powered data center campuses in the United States could produce more greenhouse gas emissions than Morocco did in 2024. These projects, associated w...

item.title: HUD halts enforcement of gender identity housing rule, citing focus on biological sex
item.description: Housing and Urban Development (HUD) Secretary Scott Turner announced that the Trump administration will stop enforcing the Obama-era Equal Access Rule of 2016, which required federally-funded housing ...

item.title: Tanzania Launches Reconciliation Commission and Criminal Investigation Following October 29 Violence
item.description: President Samia Suluhu Hassan of Tanzania has set in motion a comprehensive plan to restore national unity and address the consequences of the violent incidents that occurred on October 29. Speaking a...

item.title: Impact of Development Plans on Islamabad’s Population and Environment
item.description: Islamabad, long considered Pakistan’s elite and scenic capital, is facing dramatic changes under the current government’s development agenda. The plans for a 'new' Islamabad promise a city that blends...

item.title: Rising Gulf tensions and shifting capital flows threaten global financial stability
item.description: The article argues that escalating tensions in the Gulf, driven in part by US policy towards Iran under Donald Trump, are creating serious risks for global financial stability. A key concern is the po...

item.title: Eala and Sönmez advance to Madrid Open doubles round of 16 after solid performance
item.description: Filipina tennis standout Alex Eala, together with her doubles partner Zeynep Sönmez, successfully advanced to the round of 16 in the Madrid Open women’s doubles event. The pair delivered a composed an...

item.title: Lawal, Tinubu deliberate on security challenges and economic growth in Zamfara
item.description: Governor Dauda Lawal of Zamfara State meet with President Bola Ahmed Tinubu for inside Aso Rock Villa, Abuja, to discuss important matters wey concern security, economy and political situation for the...

item.title: Pakistan Completes Repayment of $3.45 Billion Deposits to UAE
item.description: Pakistan has successfully completed the repayment of $3.45 billion to the United Arab Emirates, as confirmed by the State Bank of Pakistan (SBP) on Friday. According to the central bank, $1 billion wa...

item.title: Steve Tisch attends Giants draft room as team makes key first-round selections
item.description: Former New York Giants co-owner Steve Tisch was spotted in the team’s draft room during the first round of the 2026 NFL Draft, drawing attention due to his evolving ownership status. Although Tisch no...

item.title: Coalition shows cautious backing for NDIS reforms amid bipartisan efforts
item.description: The Opposition Leader, Angus Taylor, has indicated that the Coalition will aim to support the Labor government's proposed changes to the National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS) as much as possible...

item.title: Shooting at Mall of Louisiana: 1 Dead, 5 Injured After Argument Between Two Groups
item.description: On April 23, 2026, a tragic incident unfolded at the Mall of Louisiana in Baton Rouge, U.S. when a violent exchange of gunfire erupted between two groups inside the food court. One person was killed, ...

item.title: Pakistan’s business confidence declines sharply amid rising costs and regional tensions
item.description: A latest Gallup Pakistan survey indicates that private sector confidence has dropped significantly during the first quarter of 2026, reflecting growing economic stress and uncertainty. The survey, bas...

item.title: ADC youths protest in Katsina, give INEC 74-hour deadline over leadership dispute
item.description: Youths under the African Democratic Congress (ADC) don raise serious concern over the party leadership matter, as dem give the Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) 74 hours to respond to t...

item.title: Congress leader petitions Supreme Court over PM Modi's Doordarshan speech during election period
item.description: On 24th April 2026, Congress functionary TN Prathapan filed a petition before the Supreme Court of India, raising concerns about the broadcast of Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s speech on 18th April vi...

item.title: RCMP Charges Consultant for Overbilling the Federal Government
item.description: The Royal Canadian Mounted Police (RCMP) has laid fraud charges against Andrew McDermott, 62, of Ottawa, and his company, AM Government Consulting Inc., following an investigation into overbilling the...

item.title: Amampondo Seek Stronger Role in Managing Wild Coast Ancestral Lands
item.description: The Amampondo community on South Africa's Wild Coast is calling for a larger role in the preservation and management of their ancestral lands. The debate is intensified as the government recently decl...

item.title: Chande Commission Identifies Causes of October 29 Unrest in Tanzania
item.description: Dar es Salaam — The Chande Commission has officially presented its findings on the October 29 violence at State House. The report says that the unrest was caused by a mix of long-standing problems and...

item.title: Trump says nuclear option not considered in potential conflict with Iran
item.description: Former US President Donald Trump has stated that he would not consider using nuclear weapons in any potential military action against Iran, easing concerns about escalation involving weapons of mass d...

item.title: FDA Approves Otarmeni™ Gene Therapy for OTOF-Related Hearing Loss, Regeneron to Provide Free in U.S.
item.description: Regeneron Pharmaceuticals announced that the FDA has granted accelerated approval for Otarmeni™ (lunsotogene parvec-cwha), marking the first approved gene therapy for OTOF-related genetic hearing loss...
